rulings.Case Title: KARENA APPLE FENG ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2026, line 1, 1 - RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS DMV's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. The petition for writ of mandate shows that the claims alleged therein are filed per CCP 1085, not 1094.5, and thus the claims are not time-barred. The petition does not show on its face that Karena's claim is barred by laches or that petitioners have an adequate legal remedy. DMV's argument in its reply brief that the petition seeks to compel a discretionary act that is not cognizable per 1085 is not considered because it was not addressed in the moving papers. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 6, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KARENA APPLE FENG ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2026, line 1, 2- PETITIONER KARENA FENG, LILIAN FENG Motion For Writ Of Mandate Petitioners' petition for writ of mandate is denied without prejudice to being re-noticed. At the time the petitioners noticed their petition for hearing the DMV's motion for judgment on the pleadings was pending and thus the notice requesting that the petition be heard on its merits is premature and not ripe. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 6, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THE GAP, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. SVES GO, LLC, A FLORIDA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Daniel Allender
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2026, line 7, PLAINTIFF THE GAP, INC'S Motion For Sanctions. Gap's motion for sanctions is denied without prejudice to being raised before the trial judge. As a pretrial discovery motion, the motion is untimely and was filed without obtaining, much less seeking, leave to be filed after the discovery motion hearing cutoff date. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 4, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BRIDGET N. RUIZ VS. JAGUAR LAND ROVER NORTH AMERICA, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2026, line 8, PLAINTIFF BRIDGET RUIZ'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendant'S Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions. Continued on the court's own motion to March 4, 2026 to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer about all remaining unresolved issues raised by the motion. The parties must meet and confer in person or by a remote video platform no later than 5pm on March 2 in a good faith effort to resolve all remaining unresolved issues raised by the motion. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARIO MONGE VS. ESSEX PORTFOLIO, L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave Of Court To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendants Essex Portfolio, L.P., A California Limited Liability Partnership; Essex Management Corporation, A California Corporation; And Essex Property Trust, Inc., A Maryland Corporation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2026, line 9, PLAINTIFF MARIO MONGE'S Motion For Leave Of Court To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendants Essex Portfolio, L.P., A California Limited Liability Partnership; Essex Management Corporation, A California Corporation; And Essex Property Trust, Inc., A Maryland Corporation Plaintiff's motion for leave to conduct financial discovery is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that there is a substantial likelihood that he will be able to prove by clear and convincing evidence that the code violations were the result of malice or oppression by the defendants. If the tentative is contested, the matter will be heard on March 6, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND COLEMAN ET AL VS. KENNETH ZHU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 27, 2026, line 10, DEFENDANTS VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., JAMES BELISLE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Transferred to be heard in department 501 on March 13, 2026. This is a real property matter per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. This motion will be heard in department 501 on March 13, 2026. That department hears all non-discovery motions in cases involving real property. Courtesy copies of all papers filed on the motion must be delivered to department 501 no later than March 3. = (302/HEK)
